questions.text: In the symbol for an electric cell, the longer line represents the:
questions.explanation: The longer line represents the positive terminal and the shorter, thicker line represents the negative terminal.

questions.text: A combination of two or more cells is called a:
questions.explanation: In a battery, the positive terminal of one cell is connected to the negative terminal of the next.

questions.text: When an electric current flows through a wire, it gets hot. This is called:
questions.explanation: This effect is used in appliances like electric heaters and irons.

questions.text: The wire used in a fuse has a:
questions.explanation: Fuse wires melt quickly and break the circuit if large currents flow through them.

questions.text: Who discovered the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.explanation: Oersted noticed that a compass needle deflected when a current-carrying wire was nearby.

questions.text: A current-carrying coil of insulated wire wrapped around a piece of iron is an:
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes a magnet as long as the current flows through the coil.
The electric bell uses an electromagnet to pull a hammer that strikes a gong.

questions.text: The thin wire inside an electric bulb that glows is the:
questions.explanation: The filament is usually made of tungsten which has a very high melting point.
MCBs are switches that automatically turn off when current in a circuit exceeds the safe limit.

questions.text: Which of the following is a safety device in electrical circuits?
questions.explanation: A fuse prevents damage to electrical circuits and possible fires.
